origin.name: Tajikistan
origin.key_document: Death certificate from ZAGS (requires certified English translation)
origin.source: FCDO Tajikistan travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Dushanbe guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation is available in Tajikistan. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option alongside full body repatriation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Death certificate from ZAGS (certified English translation required)
special_rules: Death must be registered with the civil registration office (ZAGS) promptly.
All Tajik or Russian-language documentation requires certified English translation for UK acceptance.
The Prosecutor's Office orders post-mortem for suspicious or violent deaths.
An export permit from Tajik authorities is required for all repatriations.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Dushanbe can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Tajikistan to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Tajikistan. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-4 weeks. ZAGS registration, translation, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
